title: bassia pilosa
intro: Bassia pilosa, also known as Indian knotweed or sticky weed, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayan region and parts of China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Bassia pilosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support digestive health, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Bassia pilosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
